Based on the retrieved evidence, we see detailed information about the distribution and relations of the third, fourth, sixth cranial nerves (oculomotor, trochlear, abducent respectively) as well as the first division of the fifth cranial nerve (ophthalmic division) - all of which pass through the superior orbital fissure. Amongst them, the oculomotor nerve (IIIrd cranial nerve) plays a crucial role not only due to its proximity but also because it governs many important functions like pupil constriction/dilation, eyelid elevation, and eye movement itself.
The evidence underscores that among these, the ophthalmic nerve being part of the trigeminal nerve does indeed run parallel and somewhat adjacent to key areas but isn't primarily what would be injured unless severely compressed or disrupted differently compared to the oculomotor.
Therefore, my final stance aligns strongly with option \( \boxed{A} \):
